---
title: "Analysis of Ben & Jerry’s Ice Cream Data"
author: Byeong-Hak Choe
date: 2026-02-11
categories: [ice-cream, code, data-analysis, data-visualization]
image: ben-jerry-ice-cream.png

execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
  
toc: true
---
```{r}
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(rmarkdown)
library(skimr)
library(ggthemes)
library(hrbrthemes)

theme_set(theme_minimal()+
          theme(strip.background =element_rect(fill="lightgray"),
                axis.title.x  = element_text(angle = 0,
                                            size = rel(1.5),
                                            margin = margin(10,0,0,0)),
                axis.title.y  = element_text(angle = 0,
                                            size = rel(1.5),
                                            margin = margin(0,10,0,0))
                )
          )


# Set global options for color-blind-friendly scales
scale_colour_discrete <- function(...) scale_colour_viridis_d(...)
scale_fill_discrete <- function(...) scale_fill_viridis_d(...)

```

Let's analyze the `starwars` data:

```{r}
ice_cream <- read_csv("https://bcdanl.github.io/data/ben-and-jerry-cleaned.csv")
```


```{r}
#| results: asis
rmarkdown::paged_table(ice_cream) 
```



## Variable Description for `ice_cream` data.frame

The following describes the variables in the `ice_cream` data.frame.


...


```{r}
ice_cream |> 
  skim()
```

## Flavor Description

```{r}
flavor_desc <- ice_cream |> 
  count(flavor_descr) |> 
  arrange(-n)
```


```{r}


icecream_sub <- ice_cream |> 
  filter(flavor_descr %in% flavor_desc$flavor_descr[1:5])

ggplot(icecream_sub,
       aes(x = priceper1,
           fill = flavor_descr)) +
  geom_density(alpha = .25)
```
